description_long: This object is a realistic flower with five broad, slightly pointed petals arranged in a radial pattern. The petals are a deep reddish-brown color with darker shading near the center, giving a natural gradient effect. The surface of the petals appears soft and matte, with subtle veining and gentle curvature. There is no visible stem or leaves attached, and the flower sits low and flat, as if freshly fallen or detached. The center of the flower is slightly darker, blending smoothly into the petal coloration. The overall form is symmetrical, with each petal overlapping slightly at the base.
description: A reddish-brown flower with five broad, pointed petals arranged radially and a dark center. The petals have a soft, matte finish and subtle color gradients.
description_view_0: The flower is seen from above, showing all five petals spread out symmetrically with the dark center clearly visible.
description_view_1: This angle highlights the gentle curvature and overlapping of the petals, with the leftmost petal appearing more prominent.
description_view_2: The flower is viewed from a slightly different side, emphasizing the rightmost petal and the smooth transition of color toward the center.
description_view_3: A similar side view to the previous, but the lower petal is more pronounced, and the radial symmetry remains evident.
attribute_petal_arrangement: The petals are arranged in a radial configuration, forming a symmetrical whorl with each petal slightly overlapping at the base. The arrangement is regular and evenly spaced, typical of actinomorphic flowers.
